---
layout: post
title: "Is boarding education becoming a grotesque caricature of itself?"
date: 2014-04-17
---

<div style="text-align: center;">
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;"><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," /></span></div>
<div>
<br clear="none" /></div>
<div>
<div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
<a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEi62BAPsqOAUGGrBpTCy_hd-mZbZ4InKH1YQm3ADeDGrOYRme3VqgZQicV05F2J93kY2cFxtrzhejfiWTmcuGO5KU9xHHIZSH-HMgYeg7-7t7TEoGYmCOXE6u5cT_sJ1wseyobFlO7I3cI/s1600/boarders.PNG" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="334" data-original-width="568" height="188" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEi62BAPsqOAUGGrBpTCy_hd-mZbZ4InKH1YQm3ADeDGrOYRme3VqgZQicV05F2J93kY2cFxtrzhejfiWTmcuGO5KU9xHHIZSH-HMgYeg7-7t7TEoGYmCOXE6u5cT_sJ1wseyobFlO7I3cI/s320/boarders.PNG" width="320" /></a></div>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;"><br /></span>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;"><br /></span>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">When I first started out in a posh boarding school, although I was well aware of the huge privilege my surroundings offered, there were enough 'normal' kids around to mean that the education I received was grounded in the real world.</span></div>
<div>
<br clear="none" /></div>
<div>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">True, most of my schoolmates came from determinedly middle-class backgrounds and there was a scarcity of pupils from</span> <a href="http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-22007058" shape="rect" style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot;, geneva, sans-serif;" target="_blank">'traditional working class'</a> <span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">backgrounds but there were plenty of pupils who lived in pretty modest homes and whose parents had to scrimp and save to afford (even discounted) fees.&nbsp;</span></div>
<div>
<br clear="none" /></div>
<div>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">I am concerned that this is no longer the case. Boarding fees, which hover either side of £30k even in the most modest establishments, are out of reach for the category of parents with children that I rubbed shoulders with (teachers, doctors, soldiers, accountants etc.) Increasingly the schools with these exorbitant fees are populated by the global super-rich who live in a world quite detached from normal people's reality.</span></div>
<div>
<br clear="none" /></div>
<div>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">Whereas I would have been quite happy for my children to spend their formative years in the company of 'people like us' (if you'll pardon that horrible phrase) I am much less sure I'd want him in a school where they only come into contact with the children of hedge fund managers or Russian oil oligarchs.</span></div>
<div>
<br clear="none" /></div>
<div>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">Nor is it good enough to allay these fears by pointing to a bursary/scholarship programme</span> <span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif; font-style: italic;">especially if that programme is only used to afford access to the tutored-to-the-hilt-super-boffins</span><span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">. A dose of reality and balance is needed if such schools are to avoid becoming hideous caricatures of their former selves.</span></div>
<div>
<br clear="none" /></div>
<div>
<span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">As I have explained</span> <a href="http://tjj.postach.io/in-defence-of-private-school-a-teenaged-toff-reminds-me-of-what-there-is-to-lose" shape="rect" style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot;, geneva, sans-serif;" target="_blank">here</a><span style="font-family: &quot;trebuchet ms&quot; , &quot;geneva&quot; , sans-serif;">&nbsp;independent schools are, on balance, a good thing. But there is a certain category of school - typically the 'premier league' boarding type - where a ludicrous facilities arms race has resulted in too much exclusivity. There needs to be a reality check: bin the plans for a new tartan athletics track, or Olympic-sized swimming pool (always shamefully underused in any case) and focus on the core mission of providing a good education to a healthy cross-section of pupils.&nbsp;</span></div>
<div>
<br /></div>
